questions.prompt: You designed a new ringfence for a customer's line-of-business app. What's the safe rollout pattern?
choices: Apply it Secured immediately so you find issues quickly
Apply it Monitor Only for 1-2 weeks, review Simulated denies, tune, then Secured
Apply it to the IT team's machines first
questions.explanation: Monitor Only logs would-be-blocks without enforcing. The Simulated deny rows tell you which exclusions are needed. Skipping this step is how teams accidentally block a quarterly business workflow.

questions.prompt: Which of these are levers a ThreatLocker ringfence exposes? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Application (which child processes can be spawned)
File (which paths can be read or written)
Registry (which keys can be read or modified)
questions.explanation: The four levers are application, file, network, and registry. DNS-level filtering isn't part of ringfencing; that's a different stack-slot (DNSFilter or similar).
